reviews.title: A very nice mouse.
reviews.text:
I tried to use both of the most recent Apple mouse products with my Macbook Pro and never could get the hang of either one of them. I tried configuring them in the applications folder but never could get it right plus neither one of them was comfortable. One had a ball and the other newer one did not. Usually I really like apple products but not this time. I am happy to report this Logitech worked right out of the box after I plugged in the USB thingy. i didn't even have to set it up. I do like the roller ball. I think this mouse can do more than I know about, but am just happy it isn't changing the page I am on constantly. I got the one that was painted blue and green because it was about 6.00 cheaper than the solid colored ones. I like the design now that I have it. Since my laptop only has two USB plug ins, I bought the Amazon USB hub with three additional spots. Am happy with that too.
UPDATE 11-08-14
All of a sudden I am getting these messages saying the battery is critically low. To start with I was changing the batteries every time but it was happening way too fast so I bought a battery checker. Turns out my batteries are fine. Testing 100 %. the mouse continue to operate even though I continue receiving low battery warnings. Don't know how this is going to play out but it is the first sign of trouble. Now I just take battery out. Test it and when it continues to say 100 % I put it back in and the message goes away for a couple of days. Aggravating. I am amending star count down to 4 stars. Can't change stars.

reviews.title: Works great - great little wireless mouse.
reviews.text: I got this for my daughters birthday - she loved the design.  It synced right up and worked immediately.  It worked great with her computer - she really liked he feel of the mouse.  It is easy to pack away and travel with as well.

reviews.title: The middle click and scroll wheel work great! Microsoft 4000 is smoother and more comfortable though.
reviews.text:
I own this mouse as well as the Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 now. I find them to be head to head competitors, so here's a quick comparison.

Logitech:
-Somewhat of a loud click, doesn't bother me personally, but it is obviously louder
-The harder you flick the scroll wheel the more it'll spin. There is resistance though and I feel like it's easier to use than the Microsoft mouse.
-It looks cool and functionality is fine. The middle click is easy to use and the left/right of the wheel to move back or forward in a web browser works and isn't annoyingly used by accident.

Microsoft:
-Quiet click and smoother sliding on the desk
-The wheel stops once your finger leaves it. You can't just flick and go. Scrolling seems more precise with this one, but I actually prefer the Logitech as I feel like I have more functional control with it.
-There is a side button that moves you backward in a web browser, the wheel left and right tilting doesn't seem to do anything.
-More comfortable in my hand.

Bottom line: Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 is smoother and more comfortable, but Logitech is more functional, especially for the middle click and scroll wheel. I was very skeptical about the benefits of this "free-wheel" but I love it now. If you're not a big scroll wheel and middle click enthusiast go with the Microsoft, otherwise the Logitech is for you.

reviews.title: Move along. Don't waste your time and money here.
reviews.text:
UPDATE: Two months! Two months this mouse lasted, and that was with very light use. Fully charged, I was just scrolling with it when I felt the wheel break. It just stopped making the little clicky sound/feeling. No trauma, no misuse, just very very very cheaply made. I surrender. It's time to cough up the money and buy the Apple brand mouse for my MacBook. My sanity is at least worth the $60.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:
After three previous tries, I finally got a Bluetooth mouse for my MacBook. I'm hoping this one is sturdier than the last I purchased that barely lasted six months. That remains to be seen, but given the product materials, I'm not hopeful. Making the connection after power-up or sleep mode is a little finicky. Also, a solid flat dark surface/mouse pad seems to be a prerequisite. Without it, the cursor is slow to respond or just does its own thing. I've never seen a mouse with the jitters before, but this one will cause the cursor to jiggle and twitch, even though the mouse itself it perfectly still, untouched. I've watched the cursor jitter clear off the screen. However, using a solid black mouse pad seems to have dealt with the problem. I would send it back and try a different product, but I've sent so many cheaply made and/or mislabeled mice back to Amazon in the past month, I've got return fatigue. We'll give this one a chance with the mousepad solution.

reviews.title: Won't hold a charge - must keep plugged in
reviews.text:
I've been through two of these. I returned/exchanged this first hoping it was just a defect. This mouse will only function appropriately for about 4-5 minutes tops if not directly plugged in to USB , otherwise it starts jumping/skipping/lagging etc. Plugged in it's perfect though - so if you get lucky and get one that holds a charge you'll probably be very happy with it. I just decided to keep this one and keep it plugged in. It's not ideal but it gets the job done.
I should probably mention that it is very easy to set up, basically plug and play - which was nice.

reviews.title: Loud click, nice feel on your fingers, good for small hands
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Material on your hands feels silky. Great feel.
- Scroll wheel works nicely.
- Tracking is fine.
- Battery rather than recharge, which I like because all of the recharging ones die within a year
- On/Off switch to save your battery
- Excellent for smaller hands. I wear women's gloves size S, and this fits my hands really well.

Cons:
- The click. I have a thing with clicks. It's maddening to hear yourself click loudly all day. If the click were silent, this would be the perfect mouse. Maybe I'm a little neurotic, but it's distracting for me.

Neutral:
- The LED light near your thumb is small. I don't know why so many of mice have light strips. It seems like something else to use up the battery. I'd prefer to not have any lights, outside of what's necessary underneath for a track light. Not a big deal, and better than most.

Other:
- Made In China
- 6D Optical Mouse
- Takes 2 AAA Batteries

reviews.title: Cool looking but not the best mouse
reviews.text:
I wanted a cool looking mouse to replace a Microsoft mouse that was starting to act funny, after 5 + years. After using it for an hour now I really wish I would have spent more for functionality rather than aesthetic value.

First of all I had to pull out a sheet of paper to use as a mouse pad because my old Microsoft mouse functioned with any surface that I used it on, performed flawlessly. This new mouse can't even be used on a standard wooden desk without skipping and jumping all over the screen.

I prepared myself mentally for a cheap feeling mouse because in the end you get what you pay for but holy cow this mouse feels like something I would buy at the local dollar store it screams lack of quality.

I am sure I will be buying another mouse to replace this one shortly either from frustration or because it was built at such a low quality standard.

reviews.title: Good but wanted more features
reviews.text:
I bought this in a time crunch. My wired mouse was getting hard to use because the scroll wheel had shifted out of place. I had several wireless mice around, but none of them still had their wireless receiver. And they were before the days of Bluetooth. So, I had was in the market for a new one.

This one is nice. It fits the hand well and all. But, I realized that it did not have the features that I was used to. First, it is not a quiet mouse. I did not notice at first, but when it penetrated my fog of concentration, I could not unhear it. Click. Click. Click! And, this was not great for conference calls either.

The other issue was that it tracked slower than I would like. I was still using my wired mouse at this point, so I did not want to change mouse settings via software. I really missed having a dpi button on my mouse. This is important to me as I use a laptop on an extended display to a 2nd monitor. So, I need to be able to zip from one end of the screen the other - quick enough to unmute and answer a question during a conference call.

Have to click to wake up the mouse kept catching my attention - which means it was on it's way to annoying me... I have worked with computers for so long, it's like an extension of me. It needs to be intuitive to use and not interrupt my flow.

And, at this point, I figured out that I would need to use two different computers at once. And, while I could pair this wireless mouse with a wired one, the USB ports required were a challenge. My newer computer has no USB C ports at all. So, this limited my set-up options. Secondly, I now had 2 mice crowding limited desk space.

So, I learned that there was such a thing as multi-device mice. And, I found one that can switch between two computers and a iPad - with a simple LED indicator so that you know which device you are controlling. I bought the also designed to be silent and to switch between different dpi. All these features and it was only $3 more.

This is what I bought to replace this one - Arteck Multi-Device Wireless Bluetooth Mouse with Nano USB Receiver Ergonomic Right Hand Silent Clicking for Computer Desktop PC Laptop Mac iPad and Windows 10/8 iPad OS Build in Rechargeable Battery

So, to sum it up, I would say that I need the speed and responsiveness of a gamer's mouse with the silent clicks and 3 device connects like an office worker. So, this mouse is ok. But, if you are an advance or intense user, you may find that it does meet your needs.

reviews.title: Cheap and useless.
reviews.text: This mouse feels so cheap, it wasn’t even worth giving a review but I wanted to warn others to not get this mouse. It’s so flimsy and made so cheaply, I didn’t waste anymore time with it so I am returning.

reviews.title: Flawless Bluetooth connectivity with Mac
reviews.text: My Logitech M310 (been using them for 10-15 years) could not connect properly with my USB-C/Thunderbolt MacBookPro, using their USB dongle, thru a USB adapter. Scroll wheel would NOT work, and Logitech tech support said they would not update drivers. 'Don't use an adapter.' But they aren't aware that new Macs don't have old-school USB ports? Anyone else have this problem?! Anyway... this Seenda connects directly with Bluetooth, and connects instantly. If it sleeps (after a few hours?) it wakes and re-connects with one click, in less than a second. I charged the battery when I bought it a few weeks ago, and forgot that I might need to charge it again one day. Still going. Downsides: it is much smaller and lighter than I am used to (the M310 is easily twice as thick/tall) so I am getting used to that. Took awhile to find the right DPI/resolution/speed (and the button to change that is in a precarious, easy-to-hit-by-mistake position. Overall a great recommend.

reviews.title: squeaks when scrolling - not for small hands

reviews.title: Too small to be ergonomic for large hands
reviews.text: This will be a quick return. This mouse is too small and does not have enough vertical lift to be ergonomic for someone with large hands. I was pleased with how quickly it charged. But after fully charging there is no response at all from the DPI button. And the initial setting is too slow for the mouse to be of real use. If I had kids at home I would pass this along for schoolwork, but it isn't adequate for gaming or long stretches of editing.

reviews.title: Perfect for carpal tunnel
reviews.text: If you have carpal tunnel get this now. No pain at all anymore

reviews.title: Nice vertical mouse
reviews.text: Took some time to get used to.  Once I let my hand relax and rest on the mouse, and move in a different manner, definitely feeling better overall.

reviews.title: Battery life isn't that great
reviews.text: I use this for work. 5 days a week for about 7 hours a day. I turn it off every night and turn it on every morning. I love everything about this mouse but the battery isn't that great. It died after about 2 weeks full charge

reviews.title: Returned
reviews.text: This mouse felt very awkward

title: NORMIA RITA Foldable Arc Wireless Mouse Silent Click Bluetooth 2.4GHz Dual Modes Portable Curved Mouse for Home, Office, Travel (Silver)
features: Ergonomic Mouse: Fits hands curve, arc design. Takes small space after rotate, lightweight and mini (size: 4.6"*2.3"*0.3") compact design occupies less space of your desk. A perfect fit for your hand and your mobile lifestyle. Please check the image about how to connect with the mouse.

2.4GHz & Bluetooth 4.0 dual mode: This creatively and conveniently designed mouse,can work on TWO DEVICES, connecting one device through Bluetooth and the other via RF 2.4Ghz. If you are annoying that you have to own one Bluetooth mouse and one 2.4G wireless mouse separately for your laptop and tablet, or laptop and laptop, or laptop and desktop, this mouse is your ideal choice. You could intelligently switch the mode between both devices just by a click

High Performance & Quiet Operation: Upgraded optical sensor, smooth and fast response offer you a better on-line time. Left and right buttons silent design make you work or play in a quiet environment, won’t disturb others when you at home or in the office

Energy-saving: Built-in battery, easy to charge by micro usb cable, no more need for extra battery, can be charged by computer, power bank, adapter. Can be used 15 hours continuously after fully charged.

Wide Compatibility: Passed 50,000,000 times keystroke test to guarantee extra durability. Supports Windows 7/8/10/XP, Vista 7/8 and Macbook Mac OS Linux etc. Fits for desktop, laptop, PC, Macbook and other devices.

reviews.title: Works well, light, easy.
reviews.text: This works really well, I do wish it had a little bit more weight to it.  I use the surface mouse generally and love it.  I bought this as a replacement bc I transitioned my surface mouse to my work computer. This works similarly well, it’s just so light it doesn’t feel as sturdy.  It won’t work with 2 devices at once, you have to keep switching, but does have multiple ways to connect.

reviews.title: Sharp, poor cursor control, poor scrolling
reviews.text: I had hoped, for this price, I might get something that worked...NOT!  The edges are sharp and very uncomfortable.  If you have tiny hands, this might work.  Scrolling is pretty much a bumpy ride, all or nothing, no matter how you set the scroll.  The cursor was very hard to control and very hard to get it in the right place.  Yes, I played with mouse settings and tried to tweak but no go.

reviews.title: Awesome Bluetooth function and great price!
reviews.text: After ordering two other “blue tooth” mice and having to return them both when they were dependent on a USB port, this one came through with flying colors and was cheaper than the first two I had to buy and return!!

reviews.title: Bought again
reviews.text: this mouse has everything i need it. i don’t need batteries, i can use by 2 device on a time , it can fold and the shape is very nice. good job 👍

reviews.title: Improvement over MS Arc mouse
reviews.text: Does not disconnect from laptop. Very responsive. On other hand it is rather poorly made, feels like it will fall apart at any minute.

title: 2 Sets of Hyperslides Rounded Curved Edges Mouse Feet, Mouse Skates, Pads for Logitech G305, G304 Lightspeed Wireless Gaming Mouse Feet Replacement (0.8mm, Smooth, Pure PTFE) Pro Performance Upgrade
features: ✔️ [High Quality] Replacement mouse feet that increases your control and accuracy by providing the smoothest and most consistent slide
✔️ [Buttery Smooth] Having a smoother slide helps reduce grip tension which improves accuracy and decreases wrist strain for longer and more enjoyable gaming or work sessions
✔️ [Exclusionary Tech] Our exclusive technology produces rounded edges for a scratch-free slide right out of the package; no break-in period required
✔️ [Meticulous Design] Specially calibrated 0.80 mm thickness: Provides the ideal feet height that eliminates "bottom-scraping" & provides longer life-span
✔️ [Longevity] We include 2 sets made of 100% virgin grade PTFE material for the lowest friction and eco-friendly, 3M no-residue adhesive

reviews.title: Solid Replacement
reviews.text: I was looking for replacement feet for my mouse. And I stumbled upon these. I was skeptical at first, but upon arrival I put them to the test. They feel better than the original and easy to install. Material felt good and quality was good.

title: Rii Wireless Mouse RM700 2.4G Silent Mouse with 3200 DPI,Computer Mouse with USB Nano Receiver for Laptop,PC,Mac,Chromebook,Windows and School Work(Orange)
features: Slient BUTTON: This wireless mouse with Noiseless Click, you can force on your work or study, also suitable for cafe, library or any other public place. It won’t disturb other people while using. Also it’s portable enough to take out and travel.

HIGH DPI DESIGN: This wireless mouse was built for three DPI levels(1600-2400-3200) to meet your demands. The factory default is 2400, you can kindly press the DPI button to change it. It’s only take 2 AAA battery (NOT INCLUDED) to power. This wireless mouse will turn to sleep mode in 5mins of inactivity for energy saving, can be easily activated by clicking any buttons.

PLUG AND PLAY: The USB Nano Receiver is in the battery slot, 2.4GHz Wireless provides a good connection, transmission distance up to 33ft (10m). Which eliminated delays, interference, accurate fast data transmission . There is a on/off button which enables you to turn off the mouse for energy saving. Great wireless mouse for home and office.

MAXIMUM HAND-FEELING: Slim wireless mouse shape reduces skin-friendly finish are for maximum comfort and support. The thoughtful ring and little finger rest provide extra comfort. The sturdy scroll wheel with rubber makes sure that your hand will not slip when scrolling.
WIDE COMPATILITY: This wireless mouse can be compatible with Laptop, Desktop , Notebook PC, Windows XP, Windows 8/9/10, Windows Vista, Mac OS , Chromebook.

reviews.text: good product would buy again

reviews.title: Works great
reviews.text: I've had this about a yea, works great.

title: seenda Wireless Mouse, Rechargeable Light Up Mouse for Laptop, Small Cordless Mice with Quiet Click LED Rainbow Lights for PC Computer Kids Chromebook Windows Mac,Hot Pink
features: 【Light Up Rainbow Mouse】If you are tired of the normal mouse, our wireless rainbow mouse can be a choice for you. It is super cool with 15 different led rainbow lights, and you can easily switch between led colors by pressing the bottom button. It's also a perfect gift for kids, girls,boys, students or left and right hand users

【Universal Compatibility】This wireless mouse works with Computer PC Notebook Laptop tablet with USB port, running on Windows XP, Windows Vista, Windows 7, 8,10,11, Mac OS X 10.4 , Chromebook, PS4, PS5,Xbox and more【Notice: If your laptop only have a Type C port, you will need a USB to Type C adaptor to connect it】

【Type C Recharging & Independent ON/OFF Switch】This seenda wireless mouse uses 650mAh rechargeable battery and a single charge would last for a whole week. While the intelligent auto sleep function and on/off switch help you go further between charges

【Ultra Quiet Click】We know your demands of a quiet mouse, so we have reduced the clicking and scrolling noise to up to 90% while you enjoy the same click feel. Perfect for working at home, library, students dormitory,office or even traveling. Thanks to the great ergonomic design, this mouse fits comfortably in your hand, prevent any possible wrist pain

【Driver-Free &3 Adjustable DPI】Comes with a small USB nano receiver(stored in the back of mouse), just plug it into your computer and you are ready to go. 3 Adjustable DPI levels (800/1200/1600) ,set your own cursor speed

reviews.title: Great mouse!
reviews.text: Finally a mouse that doesn't use a battery. I wasn't paying attention so it just died, but it still works while plugged into your USB. It has a great hand feel, and easy to use. I'm really happy with this.

reviews.title: nice
reviews.text: n/a

reviews.title: They buy good reviews, don't trust them. GARBAGE quality product banking on dishonesty.
reviews.text: The lights don't work well or turn off anymore after a few months when I was using a different mouse due to how often this one needed to be charged. It also CANNOT be used while being charged, so don't count on being able to actually use this often. Horrible purchase, cannot advise against them enough.

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:17a6e9e24e8e6f5bc981e3994746809]] Love this mouse! Feels great in my hand. Easy to use. Charges really fast. You can barely hear the clicks which is a plus.

10/10 would buy again.

reviews.title: Works really well so far
reviews.text: My old mouse broke so I ordered something new that I have never tried. Set up was very easy. Just plug in the little USB port wireless device that is included and the mouse already seemed to be at least partially charged so away I went immediately without issue. It comes with some very nice colors that are changeable with a push button on the bottom. I like the moving multiple colors myself. Very nice unit for the price.
